prompt.text: The post office is ___ the bank. They are very close.

answers.text: beside
next to

items.hintDe: Welche Ortspräposition heißt "daneben" und steht direkt vor the bank?
items.explainDe: beside (oder next to) heißt "neben". Direkt davor kommt das Ziel: beside the bank.
items.note: verify r1 (answers): replaced distractor 'behind' (a building directly behind another is also 'very close', so defensibly correct) with 'round the corner' (clearly NOT adjacent).

prompt.text: The restaurant is ___ the church. It is across the street.

answers.text: opposite

items.hintDe: "gegenüber" – direkt auf der anderen Straßenseite. Welche Ortspräposition passt?
items.explainDe: opposite heißt "gegenüber". Es steht ohne kleines Wort direkt vor dem Ziel: opposite the church.
items.note: SB grammar box: "The restaurant is opposite the church."

prompt.text: The cinema is ___ the music shop. Go past the music shop to find it.

answers.text: behind

items.hintDe: Das Kino liegt "hinter" dem Musikgeschäft. Welche Ortspräposition heißt das?
items.explainDe: behind heißt "hinter" und steht direkt vor dem Ziel: behind the music shop.
items.note: SB Dialogue 2: "The cinema is behind the large music shop." (large dropped — untaught at this level).

prompt.text: Don't turn here. Go ___ ahead and then turn right at the traffic lights.

answers.text: straight

items.hintDe: Bei der Wegbeschreibung heißt "geradeaus" einfach ___ ahead.
items.explainDe: Es heißt straight ahead ("geradeaus"). Erst später biegst du ab.
items.note: SB grammar box: "Go straight ahead." Distractors are real direction words, wrong before "ahead".

prompt.text: Turn ___ at the church and the hospital is on your right.

answers.text: left

items.hintDe: "Biege links ab": Nach turn kommt direkt left – ohne Präposition davor.
items.explainDe: Im Englischen sagt man Turn left – ohne to oder in davor. "Turn to left" ist falsch.
items.note: SB grammar box: "Turn left / right." Distractors are the L1 preposition-transfer error (Go in the left).

prompt.text: Go ___ the post office and the bank is on your right.

answers.text: past

items.hintDe: "an etwas vorbeigehen": Go ___ the post office. Welches kleine Wort passt?
items.explainDe: Go past heißt "an etwas vorbeigehen". Du gehst weiter, das Gebäude bleibt hinter dir.
items.note: SB grammar box: "Go past the post office." Distractors are in-bank prepositions wrong for a pass-by route.

prompt.text: You can't find the bank from here. It is just ___, beside the supermarket.

answers.text: round the corner

items.hintDe: "gleich um die Ecke" – diese feste Wendung mit corner passt hier.
items.explainDe: round the corner heißt "um die Ecke". Die drei Wörter gehören fest zusammen.
items.note: SB grammar box: "It's just round the corner, beside the bank." Answer is the whole fixed phrase.

prompt.text: There's a park ___ you. Go straight ahead and the cinema is on the right.

answers.text: in front of

items.hintDe: "vor dir" – diese Ortspräposition besteht aus drei kleinen Wörtern und endet auf of.
items.explainDe: in front of heißt "vor". Alle drei Wörter gehören zusammen: in front of you.
items.note: SB grammar box: "There's a little park in front of you." (little dropped — untaught).

prompt.text: Go to the end of the road. There's a big bank ___ George Street.

answers.text: on the corner of

items.hintDe: "an der Ecke der …-Straße": Diese Wendung benutzt on, nicht in.
items.explainDe: Man sagt on the corner of George Street ("an der Ecke der George Street"). Mit in the corner of (= drinnen in einer Ecke) ist es falsch.
items.note: verify r1 (answers): 'at the corner of' is standard idiomatic English (same meaning as 'on the corner of'), so it was a false distractor — replaced with 'to the corner of' (directional, wrong for stating location); explainDe no longer claims 'at' is wrong.

prompt.text: The cinema is across the street from the restaurant.

answers.text: The cinema is opposite the restaurant.

distractors: The cinema is opposite of the restaurant.
The cinema is opposite to the restaurant.
The cinema is opposite from the restaurant.
items.hintDe: Nach opposite kommt sofort das Ziel – ohne kleines Wort dazwischen.
items.explainDe: opposite steht direkt vor dem Ziel: opposite the restaurant. opposite of / to / from sind alle falsch.

distractorPool: The cinema is opposite of the restaurant.
The cinema is opposite to the restaurant.
The cinema is opposite from the restaurant.
The cinema is behind of the restaurant.
The cinema is beside of the restaurant.

items.note: Context line fixes the meaning; every option is in-bank, the contrast is the spurious of/to/from after opposite.

prompt.text: You want to go to the church. The traffic lights are in front of you.

answers.text: Turn left at the traffic lights.

distractors: Turn to the left at the traffic lights.
Turn in the left at the traffic lights.
Turn on the left at the traffic lights.
items.hintDe: Nach turn folgt direkt left – ohne to, in oder on davor.
items.explainDe: Im Englischen heißt es Turn left – ganz ohne Präposition. "Turn to/in/on the left" ist falsch.

distractorPool: Turn to the left at the traffic lights.
Turn in the left at the traffic lights.
Turn on the left at the traffic lights.
Turn to left at the traffic lights.
Turn on left at the traffic lights.

items.note: THE TWINS 2 line: "turn left at the traffic lights." Targets L1 preposition transfer (turn to/in/on the left).

prompt.text: directions to the railway station

answers.text: Go straight ahead and turn right at the traffic lights.

distractors: Go straight ahead and turn to the right at the traffic lights.
Go straight ahead and turn in the right at the traffic lights.
Go straight ahead and turn on the right at the traffic lights.
items.hintDe: Nach turn kommt direkt die Richtung – kein to, in oder on davor.
items.explainDe: Richtig ist turn right. Nach turn kommt kein to/in/on. straight ahead bleibt gleich.

distractorPool: Go straight ahead and turn to the right at the traffic lights.
Go straight ahead and turn in the right at the traffic lights.
Go straight ahead and turn on the right at the traffic lights.
Go straight on and turn to the right at the traffic lights.
Go straight on and turn on the right at the traffic lights.

items.note: Two-clause route; all options in-bank, the error is the spurious preposition after turn.

prompt.text: The bank is across the street from the supermarket.

answers.text: The bank is opposite the supermarket.

distractors: The bank is opposite of the supermarket.
The bank is opposite to the supermarket.
The bank is behind of the supermarket.
items.hintDe: "gegenüber": Welcher Satz benutzt opposite richtig – ganz ohne kleines Wort danach?
items.explainDe: The bank is opposite the supermarket ist richtig. opposite of / opposite to passen nicht, und behind heißt etwas anderes.

distractorPool: The bank is opposite of the supermarket.
The bank is opposite to the supermarket.
The bank is behind of the supermarket.
The bank is opposite from the supermarket.
The bank is beside of the supermarket.

items.note: Context (across the street) fixes the meaning so only the preposition surface is under test.

prompt.text: You are at the bus stop. The railway station is straight on, after the second corner on the right.

answers.text: Go straight ahead and take the second right.

distractors: Go straight ahead and turn on the second right.
Go straight ahead and turn to the second right.
Go straight ahead and turn in the second right.
items.hintDe: Für "die zweite Straße rechts" sagt man take the second right – nicht turn on/to/in.
items.explainDe: Richtig: Go straight ahead and take the second right. Bei einer nummerierten Abzweigung nimmt man take, nicht turn on/to/in.

distractorPool: Go straight ahead and turn on the second right.
Go straight ahead and turn to the second right.
Go straight ahead and turn in the second right.
Go straight on and turn on the second right.
Go straight on and turn to the second right.

items.note: SB vocabulary illustrations: "go straight ahead" + "take the second right." Contrast take vs turn for a numbered turn.

prompt.text: The hospital is opposite of the church.

answers.text: The hospital is opposite the church.
opposite the church
opposite

items.hintDe: Ein kleines Wort zu viel: Was steht hier falsch nach opposite?
items.explainDe: Nach opposite kommt kein of. Richtig: opposite the church.
items.note: Common error from the brief: opposite of → opposite. Error is in-bank words wrongly combined.

prompt.text: Turn to the left at the church.

answers.text: Turn left at the church.
Turn left

items.hintDe: Nach turn ist ein kleines Wort zu viel. Was muss weg?
items.explainDe: Es heißt Turn left – ohne to the davor. "Turn to the left" ist falsch.
items.note: Common error from the brief: L1 transfer Go in the left → Turn left.

prompt.text: The post office is behind of the bank.

answers.text: The post office is behind the bank.
behind the bank

items.hintDe: Welche Ortspräposition braucht hier kein of?
items.explainDe: behind steht direkt vor dem Ziel: behind the bank. Kein of dahinter.
items.note: Same of-insertion error class as opposite, applied to behind.

prompt.text: Go past the church and turn on the right.

answers.text: Go past the church and turn right.
turn right

items.hintDe: Nach turn ist ein kleines Wort zu viel. Was muss weg?
items.explainDe: Es heißt turn right – ohne on the davor. "turn on the right" ist falsch.
items.note: Turn-preposition error (turn on the right → turn right) after a route verb. All in-bank words, no span break.

prompt.text: traffic lights / the / turn / at / left

answers.text: Turn left at the traffic lights.
Turn left at the traffic lights

items.hintDe: Erst die Anweisung (turn + Richtung), dann wo: at the traffic lights.
items.explainDe: Turn left at the traffic lights. Nach turn kommt direkt left – die Extra-Chips to und on brauchst du nicht.

distractorPool: to
on
in
the

items.note: 5 answer chips + 2 distractor chips = 7 ≤ 10. Distractors to/on tempt the turn-to/on-left error.

prompt.text: opposite / the / cinema / is / the / restaurant

answers.text: The cinema is opposite the restaurant.
The cinema is opposite the restaurant

items.hintDe: Subjekt + is + Ortspräposition + Ziel. Nach opposite kein extra Wort.
items.explainDe: The cinema is opposite the restaurant. opposite steht direkt vor dem Ziel – of und to brauchst du nicht.

distractorPool: of
to
from
near

items.note: 6 answer chips + 2 distractor chips = 8 ≤ 10. Distractors of/to bait the opposite-of error.

prompt.text: the / past / go / and / turn / left / supermarket

answers.text: Go past the supermarket and turn left.
Go past the supermarket and turn left

items.hintDe: Zwei Anweisungen mit and verbunden: erst Go past …, dann turn left.
items.explainDe: Go past the supermarket and turn left. Nach turn kommt direkt left – ohne to oder on.

distractorPool: to
on
in
at

items.note: 7 answer chips + 2 distractor chips = 9 ≤ 10. Two-clause route, harder ordering.

prompt.text: Das Restaurant ist gegenüber der Kirche.

answers.text: The restaurant is opposite the church.
The restaurant is opposite the church
The restaurant is across from the church.

items.hintDe: "gegenüber" heißt opposite – und danach kommt direkt das Ziel, ohne of.
items.explainDe: gegenüber = opposite. Richtig: opposite the church (kein of). across from versteht man auch, aber opposite passt besser zum Lernstoff.
items.note: SB grammar box line. across from accepted as partial (correct but off-target vocabulary).

prompt.text: Geh geradeaus und biege dann rechts ab.

answers.text: Go straight ahead and then turn right.
Go straight ahead and then turn right
Go straight on and then turn right.
Go straight and then turn right.

items.hintDe: "geradeaus" = straight ahead (oder straight on), "rechts abbiegen" = turn right.
items.explainDe: Go straight ahead and then turn right. straight ahead und straight on sind beide richtig; turn right braucht kein to.
items.note: Two-clause route. straight ahead / straight on both full; bare straight is partial.

prompt.text: You are in town. You want to go to the railway station. Ask politely.

answers.text: Excuse me, where's the railway station?
Excuse me, where is the railway station?
Excuse me, can you tell me where the railway station is?
Where's the railway station?

items.hintDe: Beginne höflich mit Excuse me, dann frag nach dem Ort: where's …?
items.explainDe: Höflich fragst du: Excuse me, where's the railway station? Excuse me macht die Frage freundlich.
items.note: SB dialogues open with "Excuse me, where's the post office?" Without Excuse me it is correct but not polite → partial.

prompt.text: places and where they are

pairs.right: It's on the corner of the street.

items.hintDe: Lies jede Antwort: Welche Ortspräposition passt zu welchem Ort?
items.explainDe: Jede Antwort sagt mit einer Ortspräposition, wo der Ort ist: next to, opposite, behind, round the corner, on the corner of.
items.note: Reworked from v1 meaning-match (whose English glosses used untaught words). Q to A, both sides in-bank.

prompt.text: go straight ahead / turn left / opposite / behind

items.hintDe: Sind das Anweisungen (was tust du?) oder beschreiben sie nur den Ort (wo ist es?)?
items.explainDe: Anweisungen sagen, was du tun sollst (go, turn, cross). Ortspräpositionen sagen nur, wo etwas ist (opposite, behind, next to).
items.note: Mirrors the SB box's two halves (giving the route vs saying where). All members in-bank.
